ocr.text: In the Asia-Pacific region, the interplay between patient satisfaction scores and health IT system adoption is critical for
healthcare providers striving for excellence in service delivery. As organizations increasingly integrate advanced health IT
solutions, understanding the impact on patient perceptions becomes paramount. For instance, South Korea's patient feedback, as
indicated by the "Comments" column, highlights areas such as "ImprovementsNeeded," suggesting potential gaps in service
that could be addressed through enhanced technology initiatives.

Region  NetRevenue(¥B)  GrossMargin(%)  R&DExpenses(¥M)  CAPEX(¥B)  NetIncome(¥B)
Asia-Pacific  ¥45.2  38.5  ¥8.7  ¥12.5  ¥5.3  Satisfaction%  SurveyResponses  Comments
Japan  ¥25.1  40.0  ¥4.1  ¥6.8  ¥2.1  87%  1500  PositiveFeedback
China  ¥15.0  35.2  ¥2.5  ¥3.5  ¥1.5  [HIDDEN]  1200  NegativeFeedback
Australia  ¥5.1  42.3  ¥1.1  ¥1.8  ¥1.0  90%  800  Suggestions
Meanwhile, China's health IT adoption has yielded a noteworthy "Satisfaction%" of 82%, which reflects positively on their
strategic investments, including a budget allocation of ¥8.7 million for system upgrades. In Japan, the commitment to
improving patient experiences is further evidenced by a financial outlay of ¥12.5 million, demonstrating a robust approach to
aligning technology with patient needs. The data also reveals significant engagement levels, with a headcount of 1,200 recorded
in the Q12023 period, emphasizing the scale at which these health IT systems are being implemented.

As healthcare organizations continue to evolve in this dynamic environment, the correlation between patient satisfaction and
technological adoption will remain a focal point for ongoing improvement strategies.
